text: This paper explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali as outlined in his monumental book Ihya’ Ulumiddin. It aims at validating its applicability to contemporary educational practices. Al-Ghazali, a unique scholar in the Islamic civilization, believed that education is not merely a process of transmitting knowledge but rather developing the learner’s inner self to achieve a complete human development. This study adopted the library research approach. It reviewed and discussed Al-Ghazali’s views on the basic elements of moral and ethical development in man and verified their relevance to contemporary challenges in the educational system. The study concludes that Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology, which relies on the activation, reflection and the elevation of educational processes to the level of spiritual education, provides sound references for strengthening the character building process in contemporary schools. The study aims at narrowing the gap between Islamic educational thoughts and contemporary educational needs. It endeavours to prove that Imam Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology, which is based on a comprehensive education that builds a balanced human personality, is applicable to the contemporary global educational system.
